title: Chamaemelum macrophyllum
intro: Chamaemelum nobile, also known as chamomile or English chamomile, is a daisy-like flowering plant that belongs to the Asteraceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its calming and soothing effects, providing relief from anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues, making it a popular natural remedy for promoting relaxation and improving sleep quality. The bioactive constituents of Chamaemelum nobile include apigenin, luteolin, and chamazulene, which are responsible for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The most common herbal preparations made with chamomile include teas, infusions, tinctures, and topical creams, which are often used to soothe skin irritations and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy: Chamaemelum macrophyllum belongs to the family Asteraceae and is a member of the genus Chamaemelum. This plant is native to the Iberian Peninsula.
